title: Textile Knitting and Weaving Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that knit, loop, weave, or draw in textiles.

tasks.text: Observe woven cloth to detect weaving defects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Thread yarn, thread, and fabric through guides, needles, and rollers of machines for weaving, knitting, or other processing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Remove defects in cloth by cutting and pulling out filling.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Examine looms to determine causes of loom stoppage, such as warp filling, harness breaks, or mechanical defects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect products to ensure that specifications are met and to determine if machines need adjustment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Notify supervisors or repair staff of mechanical malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start machines, monitor operations, and make adjustments as needed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Stop machines when specified amounts of product have been produced.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect machinery to determine whether repairs are needed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Confer with co-workers to obtain information about orders, processes, or problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate machines for test runs to verify adjustments and to obtain product samples.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up, or set up and operate textile machines that perform textile processing and manufacturing operations such as winding, twisting, knitting, weaving, bonding, or stretching.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Install, level, and align machine components such as gears, chains, guides, dies, cutters, or needles to set up machinery for operation.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Record information about work completed and machine settings.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Study guides, loom patterns, samples, charts, or specification sheets, or confer with supervisors or engineering staff to determine setup requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Repair or replace worn or defective needles and other components, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean, oil, and lubricate machines, using air hoses, cleaning solutions, rags, oil cans, or grease guns.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust machine heating mechanisms, tensions, and speeds to produce specified products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
